entries.definition:
كتاب الميم

مغر - مغص

(مكن) الميم والكاف والنون كلمةٌ واحدة. المَكْن: بَيض الضَّبّ. وضَبٌّ مَكُونٌ. [قال]:

ولا تَشتهيهِ نفوسُ العَجَمْ

ومَكْنُ الضِّباب طَعامُ العُرَيبِ

m–k–n lizard and locust eggs; bird’s nest; to be exalted in
position; to be calm; to be firmly established, to consolidate, to
gain influence; to become possible. Of this root, three forms
occur 18 times in the Qur’an: مَكَّنَ makkana 13 times; أَمْكَنَ
ʾamkana once and مَكِين makīn four times.

مَكَّنَ makkana [v. II, trans.] 1 to establish, to strengthen (24:55)
وَلَيُمَكِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ دِينَهُمُ الَّذِي ارْتَضَى لَهُمْ and He will surely establish for them
their religion which He had approved for them 2 [with prep. فِي]
to settle in, to secure in (7:10) وَلَقَدْ مَكَّنَّاكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَجَعَلْنَا لَكُمْ فِيهَا مَعَايِشَ
and We have indeed settled you [people] on the earth and
provided you with a means of livelihood there 3 [with prep. لـ]
to give authority to, to give power to, to give a firm position to
(28:6) وَنُمَكِّنَ لَهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ to give them power in/over the land.

أَمْكَنَ ʾamkana [v. IV, intrans./trans.] (with no notional subject)
to become possible, to enable *(8:71) أَمْكَنَ مِنْهُمْ He had them
caught, put them in the power of their adversaries [lit. He
enabled over them].

مَكِين makīn [quasi-act. part.] 1 secure, safe (23:13) ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَاهُ
نُطْفَةً فِي قَرَارٍ مَكِينٍ then We placed him as a drop of seed in a secure
lodging 2 well established (12:54) إِنَّكَ الْيَوْمَ لَدَيْنَا مَكِينٌ أَمِينٌ you are
now well established with us and trusted.
